pi_items.source: Verified through web search: 2.5 feet (≈0.76 meters) is a standard and reasonable projection depth for vertical window shading fins in architectural design, recommended for east/west facing windows to control solar heat gain

Verified from task description and INP file analysis: Building contains 5 windows on east-facing zones (G.E12, M.E22, T.E32) and 5 windows on west-facing zones (G.W14, M.W24, T.W34). Task description explicitly specifies: 'Add vertical shading fins to all east- and west-facing windows' and 'Do NOT add fins to North or South facing windows.'

INP file analysis: Actual east-facing spaces are: B.E2 (East Perim Spc), B.E7 (East Perim Plnm), G.E12 (East Perim Spc), G.E17 (East Perim Plnm), M.E22 (East Perim Spc), M.E27 (East Perim Plnm), T.E32 (East Perim Spc), T.E37 (East Perim Plnm). Building has 4 complete floors: Below-Grade (B), Ground (G), Middle/Above-Grade (M), and Top (T), but metadata omits the Below-Grade floor prefix.

INP file analysis: Actual west-facing spaces are: B.W4 (West Perim Spc), B.W9 (West Perim Plnm), G.W14 (West Perim Spc), G.W19 (West Perim Plnm), M.W24 (West Perim Spc), M.W29 (West Perim Plnm), T.W34 (West Perim Spc), T.W39 (West Perim Plnm). Building has 4 complete floors, but metadata omits the Below-Grade floor prefix.

Verified: Correct Windows path for the Docker/Windows 11 environment as specified in env.json. The actual building model file exists at /examples/equest_env/data/4StoreyBuilding.inp and is mapped to C:\workspace\tasks in the Windows environment.
Verified: Correct Windows path pattern. .SIM files are standard DOE-2 simulation output files generated after running an eQUEST simulation, as confirmed by DOE-2 documentation.

privileged_info_summary: The 4StoreyBuilding is a synthetic eQUEST building model with 4 complete floors (Below-Grade, Ground, Middle, Top), containing 20 total windows across East (5), West (5), North (5), and South (5) orientations distributed across ground and above-ground floors only (no basement windows). The target fin depth of 2.5 feet is a verified standard projection depth for vertical window shading fins used to reduce solar heat gain on east/west facades. The metadata zone lists (zones_east and zones_west) are incomplete, omitting the Below-Grade floor prefix (B.E and B.W), though this has no practical impact since no windows exist in basement zones. eQUEST version 3.65 is the DOE-2.2 building energy simulation tool for Windows, using .INP files for building models and .SIM files for simulation outputs.
